name: Embalse de González Lacasa, Rasillo en Cameros, Pradillo – circular
region_intro: Mountain bike trails Embalse González-Lacasa are set within La Rioja, Spain, at an altitude of 1,000 meters, surrounded by extensive pine forests and mountains. The terrain offers varied landscapes, including challenging climbs and scenic views, with some mountains exceeding 2,000 meters. This elevated and mountainous environment provides a diverse backdrop for mountain biking. The area around the reservoir features a mix of forests and rivers, creating engaging routes for riders. Best mountain bike trails around Embalse González-Lacasa The most popular mountain bike trail is Embalse de González Lacasa, Rasillo en Cameros, Pradillo – circular, a 21.6 miles (34.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 54 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers varied landscapes around the reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Pradillo Village – González Lacasa Reservoir loop from El Rasillo de Cameros, a moderate 18.0 miles (29.0 km) path. This loop provides scenic views of the reservoir and surrounding pine forests. Local mountain bikers also love the El Rasillo de Cameros – Church of San Martín loop from El Rasillo de Cameros, a 25.7 miles (41.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 58 minutes. Mountain biking around Embalse González-Lacasa is defined by pine forests, mountainous terrain, and reservoir views. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult climbs. The routes in Embalse González-Lacasa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Embalse González-Lacasa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Embalse González-Lacasa?
A: There are 27 mountain bike trails around Embalse González-Lacasa. These routes offer a mix of moderate and difficult options, catering to various skill levels.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for families or beginners?
A: While the area features 12 moderate and 15 difficult mountain bike trails, there are no routes specifically categorized as easy. However, some moderate routes, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993748385/embalse-de-gonzalez-lacasa-rasillo-en-cameros-pradillo-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de González Lacasa, Rasillo en Cameros, Pradillo – circular&lt;/a&gt;, offer varied landscapes around the reservoir that might be suitable for families with some biking experience.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails around Embalse González-Lacasa?
A: While specific trail regulations for dogs vary, the Embalse González-Lacasa area is generally welcoming to pets. The Club Náutico El Rasillo even features a dedicated bathing area for dogs, suggesting a dog-friendly environment. Always ensure your dog is under control and check local signage for any restrictions on specific trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails?
A: The terrain around Embalse González-Lacasa is diverse, featuring extensive pine forests, mountainous landscapes, and views of the reservoir. You can expect varied surfaces, including challenging climbs and descents, as well as scenic paths through forests and alongside rivers. Some mountains in the region exceed 2,000 meters, offering significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around the reservoir?
A: Yes, there are several circular mountain bike routes. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20149811" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pradillo Village – González Lacasa Reservoir loop from El Rasillo de Cameros&lt;/a&gt;, which provides scenic views of the reservoir and surrounding pine forests. Another is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993748385/embalse-de-gonzalez-lacasa-rasillo-en-cameros-pradillo-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de González Lacasa, Rasillo en Cameros, Pradillo – circular&lt;/a&gt;, offering varied landscapes around the reservoir.

Q: What other activities can I do near Embalse González-Lacasa besides mountain biking?
A: Beyond mountain biking, Embalse González-Lacasa is a hub for water activities. The Club Náutico El Rasillo offers pedaloes, kayaking, stand-up paddleboarding (SUP), windsurfing, and sailing. You can also enjoy swimming in designated areas, some with floating platforms, slides, and trampolines. The area is also excellent for nature observation due to its immense flora and fauna.

Q: Where can I find parking when visiting Embalse González-Lacasa for mountain biking?
A: The Club Náutico El Rasillo, located on the reservoir, provides convenient parking facilities. Access to the embalse and its amenities, including parking, is generally free of charge.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Embalse González-Lacasa?
A: Given its altitude of 1,000 meters and mountainous environment, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking, with milder temperatures. Summers can be warm, while winters might bring colder temperatures and potentially snow at higher elevations, impacting trail conditions.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the Club Náutico El Rasillo, situated directly on the reservoir, features both a bar and a restaurant. These facilities provide convenient options for refreshments and meals after your ride.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Embalse González-Lacasa?
A: The mountain bike trails in Embalse González-Lacasa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, scenic views of the reservoir, and the extensive pine forests that define the routes.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, the area offers 15 difficult mountain bike routes for experienced riders. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rasillo de Cameros – Church of San Martín loop from El Rasillo de Cameros&lt;/a&gt; is a 41.3 km trail leading through varied and challenging terrain. Another demanding option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40009346" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Hincada Pass – Ortigosa de Cameros loop from Ortigosa de Cameros&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation changes.

Q: What facilities are available at Embalse González-Lacasa?
A: The Embalse González-Lacasa offers several convenient facilities, particularly at the Club Náutico El Rasillo. These include a bar, restaurant, parking, public restrooms, and a Red Cross first-aid post. There are also adapted ramps for accessibility and a dedicated bathing area for dogs.

Q: How long do the mountain bike trails typically take to complete?
A: The duration of mountain bike trails around Embalse González-Lacasa varies depending on the route and your pace. For instance, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993748385/embalse-de-gonzalez-lacasa-rasillo-en-cameros-pradillo-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de González Lacasa, Rasillo en Cameros, Pradillo – circular&lt;/a&gt; typically takes about 2 hours and 54 minutes, while the more difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rasillo de Cameros – Church of San Martín loop from El Rasillo de Cameros&lt;/a&gt; can take around 3 hours and 58 minutes.
tips: Better known by local people as “El swamp del Rasillo” or “El Pantano de Ortigosa”, it is located at an altitude of 1,000 meters, surrounded by pine forests, in the Sierra de Cameros in La Rioja. There are good views, and the possibility of bathing, sunbathing, eating and having a drink.

El Rasillo is a town in Los Cameros that has a livestock tradition. Today it is a tourist town located between the swamp and the mountain.

A very special corner that deserves a visit on wheels.
Little town of Cameros in the middle of national 111, on the way to the port of Piqueras.

Villoslada de Cameros is an urban center crossed by the Río Mayor, which is located in the extreme south of La Rioja, at the foot of the Sierra de Cebollera Natural Park. It has an interesting architectural urban area.

waypoints.name: Villoslada de Cameros Historic Village and Stone Bridge
